title: Do not collapse `MLF.Reify.Core`'s local `solvedFromView`/`...Solved` seam yet
problem: `TODO.md:100` says to keep `fromSolved`/`solvedFromView` local and only keep shrinking those seams when their callers disappear. In `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:82`, the real reify engine (`reifyWith`) still consumes a `Solved`-shaped environment, and its binder-order / dependency logic still calls `Order.orderKeysFromRoot solved` and `freeVars solved` at `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:607` and `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:876`. The owner-local wrappers at `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:703`, `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:734`, `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:756`, `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:787`, `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:815`, plus `solvedFromView` at `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:867`, are therefore not dead compatibility shims; they are the current adapter boundary around a still-`Solved` core. `src/MLF/Util/Order.hs:40` reinforces that shape by still exposing `orderKeysFromRoot :: Solved -> ...`.
why_this_is_simpler: This is not materially simpler right now. The public/runtime cleanup already happened on 2026-03-07 and 2026-03-08 (`implementation_notes.md:169`, `implementation_notes.md:39`): `PresolutionView` is the non-legacy API, and the public `Solved` facade has already been trimmed. Removing this remaining local cluster would not delete a cross-module seam; it would force a deeper internal rewrite of graph-sensitive reification plumbing just to save a handful of owner-local wrappers. That is more churn than simplification.
why_thesis_exactness_should_be_preserved: Reification here is the live Schi/Schi_p / `T(n)` path (`src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:691`, `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:785`), so the critical invariant is preserving the exact split between original-constraint queries and canonical-constraint queries. The current local adapter reconstructs a `Solved` from `PresolutionView` using `pvConstraint`, `pvCanonicalMap`, and `pvCanonicalConstraint` at `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:867`, matching the view boundary semantics in `src/MLF/Constraint/Presolution/View.hs:24`. A direct view-native rewrite would need to prove it still uses the original graph for order/free-variable traversal and the canonical graph for weakened/eliminated/binder behavior; until that rewrite is needed for a stronger reason, leaving the local seam in place is the safer thesis-exact choice.
expected_benefits: At most, this would remove a little owner-local adapter code and one local use of `Solved.Internal`.
It could eventually make the internal reify plumbing mirror the already-view-native public signatures more directly.
It would slightly reduce local naming noise if a later change already forces `reifyWith` off `Solved`.
risks: Low payoff: the seam is already confined to one owner module, so deleting it buys little architectural simplification.

Moderate semantic risk: `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:97` and `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:98` deliberately separate original and canonical constraints, and that split is easy to disturb during a view-native rewrite.
Scope creep: removing `solvedFromView` cleanly likely pulls in `src/MLF/Util/Order.hs:40` and a parallel rewrite of `freeVars` logic at `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:876`.
This pushes on a graph-sensitive module that current guidance explicitly treats conservatively, so it is easy to spend a round for mostly stylistic payoff.
required_validation: No change recommended in round 1; if revisited later, first prove that the `Solved`-shaped callers have actually disappeared rather than being merely owner-local.

If someone does pursue it, add focused regressions showing direct-view reify matches current behavior for named-set, no-fallback, and bound reification paths rooted in `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:701`, `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:729`, and `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs:785`.
Re-run the existing view/surface guards: `test/PipelineSpec.hs:474` and the `PresolutionView mirrors solved canonical/node/bound queries` coverage referenced at `implementation_notes.md:47`.
Before claiming success, run the normal thesis-exact gate: `cabal build all && cabal test`.
